[[sn:Yamato Kofun Group]] [[ja:еӨ§е’ҢеҸӨеўізҫӨ]] [[uk:РЎРәСғРҝСҮРөРҪРҪСҸ РәСғСҖРіР°РҪС–РІ РһСҸРјР°СӮРҫ]]
<!--Yamato Kofun Group--><!--Yamato Kofun Group--><!--Yamato Kofun Group--><!--Yamato Kofun Group--><!--Yamato Kofun Group--><!--Yamato Kofun Group--><!--Yamato Kofun Group--><!--Yamato Kofun Group--> [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:ЕҢyamato Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:History of Tenri, Nara]]
[[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[sn:ЕҢyamato Kofun Cluster]]

{{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tenri|qid=Q502196}}|part_of={{ill|Hiezuka Kofun|qid=Q30923506}}; {{ill|Higashitonozuka Kofun|qid=Q11527550}}; {{ill|Nakayama ЕҢtsuka Kofun|qid=Q11364125}}; {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|qid=Q11628944}}; {{ill|Nishiyamazuka Kofun|qid=Q11627823}}; {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|qid=Q11361070}}|Historic Site={{ill|Historic Site of Japan|qid=Q30834580}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11433419]] Wikidata name: ЕҢyamato Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:42, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Yamato Kofun Group''' (еӨ§е’ҢеҸӨеўізҫӨ) is a [[Kofun Group]] located in the southern part of [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara Prefecture]] (иҗұз”ҹз”ә to дёӯеұұз”ә). Some parts of the group have been designated as [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}.
It is also referred to as "'''ЕҢyamato Kofun Cluster'''." The largest kofun is {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|иҘҝж®ҝеЎҡеҸӨеўі|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}}. Along the southeastern foothills of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]], three Kofun GroupsвҖ”{{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|lt=Makimuku Kofun Cluster|lt=Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|зәҸеҗ‘еҸӨеўізҫӨ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to зә’еҗ‘еҸӨеўізҫӨ}}, {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Group|ja|жҹіжң¬еҸӨеўізҫӨ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Group|qq=}}, and Yamato Kofun GroupвҖ”are located in a north-south alignment along the right bank of the {{ill|Hase River (Nara Prefecture)|ja|еҲқзҖ¬е·қ (еҘҲиүҜзңҢ)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hase River (Nara Prefecture)|qq=|1=Hasegawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123408533}}. Although further divided into three smaller groups, they can collectively be referred to as the "ЕҢyamato Kofun Cluster" in its broadest sense.
Excluding the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|зәҸеҗ‘еҸӨеўізҫӨ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Cluster|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to зә’еҗ‘еҸӨеўізҫӨ}} (also known as Hashimoto Kofun Group), which contains early [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] such as [[:en:Hashihaka Kofun|Hashihaka Kofun]], the Yamato Kofun Group (in the narrower sense) and the Yanagimoto Kofun Group are considered to have been constructed slightly later. Together, they are sometimes called the '''ЕҢyamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group'''. These kofun are believed to have been created during the early Kofun period.
== Overview ==
The group is referred to as the "Yamato Kofun Group" due to the presence of the [[:en:ЕҢyamato Shrine|ЕҢyamato Shrine]] at the western edge of the kofun cluster. Within the group, there are 12 known [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], 5 {{ill|ZenpЕҚ-kЕҚhЕҚ-fun|ja|еүҚж–№еҫҢж–№еўі|lt=ZenpЕҚ-kЕҚhЕҚ-fun|1=ZenpЕҚ-kЕҚhЕҚ-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpЕҚ-kЕҚhЕҚ-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ZenpЕҚ-kЕҚhЕҚ-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpЕҚ-kЕҚhЕҚ-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and 7 {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|еҶҶеўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. These tombs can be further divided into two sub-groups: one on the hills (Nakayama subgroup) and one on the alluvial fan (KayЕҚ subgroup). Accordingly, the names "Nakayama Kofun Group" and "KayЕҚ Kofun Group" are often used.
The Yamato Kofun Group lacks significant size differences among the tombs, distinguishing it from the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Group|ja|жҹіжң¬еҸӨеўізҫӨ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Group|qq=}}, which emphasizes primary tombs and subsidiary tombs, and the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|lt=Makimuku Kofun Cluster|lt=Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|зәҸеҗ‘еҸӨеўізҫӨ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to зә’еҗ‘еҸӨеўізҫӨ}}, which also features a primary-subsidiary tomb relationship. Notably, subsidiary tombs do not involve forced burial practices.
The largest keyhole-shaped kofun within the Yamato Kofun Group, the {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|иҘҝж®ҝеЎҡеҸӨеўі|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}} (mound length of 234 meters), is thought to have been constructed earlier than the {{ill|Andon'yama Kofun|ja|иЎҢзҮҲеұұеҸӨеўі|1=Andon-yama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon'yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}} (241 meters, currently designated as the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]) and the {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|жёӢи°·еҗ‘еұұеҸӨеўі|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} (310 meters, currently designated as the tomb of [[:en:Emperor KeikЕҚ|Emperor KeikЕҚ]]) in the Yanagimoto Kofun Group.
Large tombs believed to be royal burial sites moved to the northwestern part of the Nara Basin by the latter half of the early Kofun period, forming the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|ja|дҪҗзҙҖзӣҫеҲ—еҸӨеўізҫӨ|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}}.
In [[:en:2014|2014]] (October 6), the burial areas of the Nomugi Kofun, {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|дёӢжұ еұұеҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikeyama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Nakayama ЕҢtsuka Kofun|ja|дёӯеұұеӨ§еЎҡеҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama ЕҢtsuka Kofun|qq=}} were collectively designated as a National Historic Site under the name "Yamato Kofun Group."<ref>{{еӣҪжҢҮе®ҡж–ҮеҢ–иІЎзӯүгғҮгғјгӮҝгғҷгғјгӮ№|401|00003861|еӨ§е’ҢеҸӨеўізҫӨ|1=Yamato Kofun Group}}</ref>
== Main Kofun ==
The largest tomb in the Yamato Kofun Group is the aforementioned {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|иҘҝж®ҝеЎҡеҸӨеўі|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}}, classified as a "great king tomb." Currently designated as the burial site of [[:en:Princess Tashiraka|Princess Tashiraka]], it is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]], restricting public and academic access without special permission. This designation was made in [[:en:1876|1876]], although Te-shirakano-hime (consort of [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]]) lived during the first half of the [[:en:6th century|6th century]]. However, the estimated construction period of the tomb dates back to the late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]] to the early [[:en:4th century|4th century]], suggesting it served as a royal tomb during the early [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato Kingship]].
Nearby, the {{ill|Higashitonozuka Kofun|ja|жқұж®ҝеЎҡеҸӨеўі|1=Higashitonotsuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashitonotsuka Kofun|qq=}} (175 meters) belongs to the KayЕҚ subgroup and is thought to have been constructed in the early 4th century.
Other significant kofun include seven keyhole-shaped kofun measuring 100вҖ“140 meters and three keyhole-shaped tombs with rectangular backs.
The {{ill|Nakayama ЕҢtsuka Kofun|ja|дёӯеұұеӨ§еЎҡеҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama ЕҢtsuka Kofun|qq=}} (120 meters) contains fragments of {{ill|special pedestal-shaped vessels|ja|зү№ж®ҠеҷЁеҸ°|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/special pedestal-shaped vessels|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to зү№ж®ҠеҷЁеҸ°гғ»зү№ж®ҠеЈә|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570998}}, indicating a possible origin in the early Kofun period, as noted in 1986. In 1993, a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|з«Әз©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=з«Әз©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө|4=zh|5=иұҺз©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} was discovered in the rear circular mound. This chamber features slab stones primarily composed of pyroxene andesite (identified as {{ill|Sanukite|ja|гӮөгғҢгӮ«гӮӨгғҲ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanukite|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to и®ғеІҗеІ©|1=Sanukitoid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q843420}}).
The {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|иҘҝеұұеЎҡеҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}}, which is speculated to be the true tomb of Te-shirakano-hime, is the only tomb within this group dating to the first half of the 6th century.

Nearby, the {{ill|Higashitonozuka Kofun|ja|жқұж®ҝеЎҡеҸӨеўі|1=Higashitonotsuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashitonotsuka Kofun|qq=}} (175 meters) belongs to the KayЕҚ subgroup and is thought to have been constructed in the early 4th century.
Other significant kofun include seven keyhole-shaped kofun measuring 100вҖ“140 meters and three keyhole-shaped tombs with rectangular backs.
The {{ill|Nakayama ЕҢtsuka Kofun|ja|дёӯеұұеӨ§еЎҡеҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama ЕҢtsuka Kofun|qq=}} (120 meters) contains fragments of {{ill|special pedestal-shaped vessels|ja|зү№ж®ҠеҷЁеҸ°|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/special pedestal-shaped vessels|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to зү№ж®ҠеҷЁеҸ°гғ»зү№ж®ҠеЈә|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570998}}, indicating a possible origin in the early Kofun period, as noted in 1986. In 1993, a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|з«Әз©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=з«Әз©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө|4=zh|5=иұҺз©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} was discovered in the rear circular mound. This chamber features slab stones primarily composed of pyroxene andesite (identified as {{ill|Sanukite|ja|гӮөгғҢгӮ«гӮӨгғҲ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanukite|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to и®ғеІҗеІ©|1=Sanukitoid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q843420}}).
The {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|иҘҝеұұеЎҡеҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}}, which is speculated to be the true tomb of Te-shirakano-hime, is the only tomb within this group dating to the first half of the 6th century.

{{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tenri|qid=Q502196}}|part_of={{ill|Hiezuka Kofun|qid=Q30923506}}; {{ill|Higashitonozuka Kofun|qid=Q11527550}}; {{ill|Nakayama ЕҢtsuka Kofun|qid=Q11364125}}; {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|qid=Q11628944}}; {{ill|Nishiyamazuka Kofun|qid=Q11627823}}; {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|qid=Q11361070}}|Historic Site={{ill|Historic Site of Japan|qid=Q30834580}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11433419]] Wikidata name: ЕҢyamato Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:42, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Yamato Kofun Group''' (еӨ§е’ҢеҸӨеўізҫӨ) is a [[Kofun Group]] located in the southern part of [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara Prefecture]] (иҗұз”ҹз”ә to дёӯеұұз”ә). Some parts of the group have been designated as [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}.
It is also referred to as "'''ЕҢyamato Kofun Cluster'''." The largest kofun is {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|иҘҝж®ҝеЎҡеҸӨеўі|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}}. Along the southeastern foothills of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]], three Kofun GroupsвҖ”{{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|lt=Makimuku Kofun Cluster|lt=Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|зәҸеҗ‘еҸӨеўізҫӨ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to зә’еҗ‘еҸӨеўізҫӨ}}, {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Group|ja|жҹіжң¬еҸӨеўізҫӨ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Group|qq=}}, and Yamato Kofun GroupвҖ”are located in a north-south alignment along the right bank of the {{ill|Hase River (Nara Prefecture)|ja|еҲқзҖ¬е·қ (еҘҲиүҜзңҢ)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hase River (Nara Prefecture)|qq=|1=Hasegawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123408533}}. Although further divided into three smaller groups, they can collectively be referred to as the "ЕҢyamato Kofun Cluster" in its broadest sense.
Excluding the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|зәҸеҗ‘еҸӨеўізҫӨ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Cluster|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to зә’еҗ‘еҸӨеўізҫӨ}} (also known as Hashimoto Kofun Group), which contains early [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] such as [[:en:Hashihaka Kofun|Hashihaka Kofun]], the Yamato Kofun Group (in the narrower sense) and the Yanagimoto Kofun Group are considered to have been constructed slightly later. Together, they are sometimes called the '''ЕҢyamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group'''. These kofun are believed to have been created during the early Kofun period.
== Overview ==
The group is referred to as the "Yamato Kofun Group" due to the presence of the [[:en:ЕҢyamato Shrine|ЕҢyamato Shrine]] at the western edge of the kofun cluster. Within the group, there are 12 known [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], 5 {{ill|ZenpЕҚ-kЕҚhЕҚ-fun|ja|еүҚж–№еҫҢж–№еўі|lt=ZenpЕҚ-kЕҚhЕҚ-fun|1=ZenpЕҚ-kЕҚhЕҚ-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpЕҚ-kЕҚhЕҚ-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ZenpЕҚ-kЕҚhЕҚ-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpЕҚ-kЕҚhЕҚ-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and 7 {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|еҶҶеўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. These tombs can be further divided into two sub-groups: one on the hills (Nakayama subgroup) and one on the alluvial fan (KayЕҚ subgroup). Accordingly, the names "Nakayama Kofun Group" and "KayЕҚ Kofun Group" are often used.
The Yamato Kofun Group lacks significant size differences among the tombs, distinguishing it from the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Group|ja|жҹіжң¬еҸӨеўізҫӨ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Group|qq=}}, which emphasizes primary tombs and subsidiary tombs, and the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|lt=Makimuku Kofun Cluster|lt=Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|зәҸеҗ‘еҸӨеўізҫӨ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to зә’еҗ‘еҸӨеўізҫӨ}}, which also features a primary-subsidiary tomb relationship. Notably, subsidiary tombs do not involve forced burial practices.
The largest keyhole-shaped kofun within the Yamato Kofun Group, the {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|иҘҝж®ҝеЎҡеҸӨеўі|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}} (mound length of 234 meters), is thought to have been constructed earlier than the {{ill|Andon'yama Kofun|ja|иЎҢзҮҲеұұеҸӨеўі|1=Andon-yama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon'yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}} (241 meters, currently designated as the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]) and the {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|жёӢи°·еҗ‘еұұеҸӨеўі|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} (310 meters, currently designated as the tomb of [[:en:Emperor KeikЕҚ|Emperor KeikЕҚ]]) in the Yanagimoto Kofun Group.
Large tombs believed to be royal burial sites moved to the northwestern part of the Nara Basin by the latter half of the early Kofun period, forming the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|ja|дҪҗзҙҖзӣҫеҲ—еҸӨеўізҫӨ|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}}.
In [[:en:2014|2014]] (October 6), the burial areas of the Nomugi Kofun, {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|дёӢжұ еұұеҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikeyama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Nakayama ЕҢtsuka Kofun|ja|дёӯеұұеӨ§еЎҡеҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama ЕҢtsuka Kofun|qq=}} were collectively designated as a National Historic Site under the name "Yamato Kofun Group."<ref>{{еӣҪжҢҮе®ҡж–ҮеҢ–иІЎзӯүгғҮгғјгӮҝгғҷгғјгӮ№|401|00003861|еӨ§е’ҢеҸӨеўізҫӨ|1=Yamato Kofun Group}}</ref>
== Main Kofun ==
The largest tomb in the Yamato Kofun Group is the aforementioned {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|иҘҝж®ҝеЎҡеҸӨеўі|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}}, classified as a "great king tomb." Currently designated as the burial site of [[:en:Princess Tashiraka|Princess Tashiraka]], it is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]], restricting public and academic access without special permission. This designation was made in [[:en:1876|1876]], although Te-shirakano-hime (consort of [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]]) lived during the first half of the [[:en:6th century|6th century]]. However, the estimated construction period of the tomb dates back to the late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]] to the early [[:en:4th century|4th century]], suggesting it served as a royal tomb during the early [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato Kingship]].
Nearby, the {{ill|Higashitonozuka Kofun|ja|жқұж®ҝеЎҡеҸӨеўі|1=Higashitonotsuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashitonotsuka Kofun|qq=}} (175 meters) belongs to the KayЕҚ subgroup and is thought to have been constructed in the early 4th century.
Other significant kofun include seven keyhole-shaped kofun measuring 100вҖ“140 meters and three keyhole-shaped tombs with rectangular backs.
The {{ill|Nakayama ЕҢtsuka Kofun|ja|дёӯеұұеӨ§еЎҡеҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama ЕҢtsuka Kofun|qq=}} (120 meters) contains fragments of {{ill|special pedestal-shaped vessels|ja|зү№ж®ҠеҷЁеҸ°|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/special pedestal-shaped vessels|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to зү№ж®ҠеҷЁеҸ°гғ»зү№ж®ҠеЈә|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570998}}, indicating a possible origin in the early Kofun period, as noted in 1986. In 1993, a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|з«Әз©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=з«Әз©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө|4=zh|5=иұҺз©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} was discovered in the rear circular mound. This chamber features slab stones primarily composed of pyroxene andesite (identified as {{ill|Sanukite|ja|гӮөгғҢгӮ«гӮӨгғҲ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanukite|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to и®ғеІҗеІ©|1=Sanukitoid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q843420}}).
The {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|иҘҝеұұеЎҡеҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}}, which is speculated to be the true tomb of Te-shirakano-hime, is the only tomb within this group dating to the first half of the 6th century.

Together, they are sometimes called the '''ЕҢyamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group'''. These kofun are believed to have been created during the early Kofun period.

The group is referred to as the "Yamato Kofun Group" due to the presence of the [[:en:ЕҢyamato Shrine|ЕҢyamato Shrine]] at the western edge of the kofun cluster. Notably, subsidiary tombs do not involve forced burial practices.
The largest keyhole-shaped kofun within the Yamato Kofun Group, the {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|иҘҝж®ҝеЎҡеҸӨеўі|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}} (mound length of 234 meters), is thought to have been constructed earlier than the {{ill|Andon'yama Kofun|ja|иЎҢзҮҲеұұеҸӨеўі|1=Andon-yama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon'yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}} (241 meters, currently designated as the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]) and the {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|жёӢи°·еҗ‘еұұеҸӨеўі|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} (310 meters, currently designated as the tomb of [[:en:Emperor KeikЕҚ|Emperor KeikЕҚ]]) in the Yanagimoto Kofun Group.
Large tombs believed to be royal burial sites moved to the northwestern part of the Nara Basin by the latter half of the early Kofun period, forming the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|ja|дҪҗзҙҖзӣҫеҲ—еҸӨеўізҫӨ|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}}.
In [[:en:2014|2014]] (October 6), the burial areas of the Nomugi Kofun, {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|дёӢжұ еұұеҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikeyama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Nakayama ЕҢtsuka Kofun|ja|дёӯеұұеӨ§еЎҡеҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama ЕҢtsuka Kofun|qq=}} were collectively designated as a National Historic Site under the name "Yamato Kofun Group."<ref>{{еӣҪжҢҮе®ҡж–ҮеҢ–иІЎзӯүгғҮгғјгӮҝгғҷгғјгӮ№|401|00003861|еӨ§е’ҢеҸӨеўізҫӨ|1=Yamato Kofun Group}}</ref>
== Main Kofun ==
The largest tomb in the Yamato Kofun Group is the aforementioned {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|иҘҝж®ҝеЎҡеҸӨеўі|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}}, classified as a "great king tomb." Currently designated as the burial site of [[:en:Princess Tashiraka|Princess Tashiraka]], it is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]], restricting public and academic access without special permission. This designation was made in [[:en:1876|1876]], although Te-shirakano-hime (consort of [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]]) lived during the first half of the [[:en:6th century|6th century]]. However, the estimated construction period of the tomb dates back to the late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]] to the early [[:en:4th century|4th century]], suggesting it served as a royal tomb during the early [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato Kingship]].
Nearby, the {{ill|Higashitonozuka Kofun|ja|жқұж®ҝеЎҡеҸӨеўі|1=Higashitonotsuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashitonotsuka Kofun|qq=}} (175 meters) belongs to the KayЕҚ subgroup and is thought to have been constructed in the early 4th century.
Other significant kofun include seven keyhole-shaped kofun measuring 100вҖ“140 meters and three keyhole-shaped tombs with rectangular backs.
The {{ill|Nakayama ЕҢtsuka Kofun|ja|дёӯеұұеӨ§еЎҡеҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama ЕҢtsuka Kofun|qq=}} (120 meters) contains fragments of {{ill|special pedestal-shaped vessels|ja|зү№ж®ҠеҷЁеҸ°|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/special pedestal-shaped vessels|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to зү№ж®ҠеҷЁеҸ°гғ»зү№ж®ҠеЈә|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570998}}, indicating a possible origin in the early Kofun period, as noted in 1986. In 1993, a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|з«Әз©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=з«Әз©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө|4=zh|5=иұҺз©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} was discovered in the rear circular mound. This chamber features slab stones primarily composed of pyroxene andesite (identified as {{ill|Sanukite|ja|гӮөгғҢгӮ«гӮӨгғҲ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanukite|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to и®ғеІҗеІ©|1=Sanukitoid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q843420}}).
The {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|иҘҝеұұеЎҡеҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}}, which is speculated to be the true tomb of Te-shirakano-hime, is the only tomb within this group dating to the first half of the 6th century.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:еӨ§е’ҢеҸӨеўізҫӨ]][[uk:РЎРәСғРҝСҮРөРҪРҪСҸ РәСғСҖРіР°РҪС–РІ РһСҸРјР°СӮРҫ]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tenri|qid=Q502196}}|part_of={{ill|Hiezuka Kofun|qid=Q30923506}}; {{ill|Higashitonozuka Kofun|qid=Q11527550}}; {{ill|Nakayama ЕҢtsuka Kofun|qid=Q11364125}}; {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|qid=Q11628944}}; {{ill|Nishiyamazuka Kofun|qid=Q11627823}}; {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|qid=Q11361070}}|Historic Site={{ill|Historic Site of Japan|qid=Q30834580}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11433419]] Wikidata name: ЕҢyamato Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:42, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Yamato Kofun Group''' (еӨ§е’ҢеҸӨеўізҫӨ) is a [[Kofun Group]] located in the southern part of [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara Prefecture]] (иҗұз”ҹз”ә to дёӯеұұз”ә). Some parts of the group have been designated as [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}.
It is also referred to as "'''ЕҢyamato Kofun Cluster'''." The largest kofun is {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|иҘҝж®ҝеЎҡеҸӨеўі|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}}. Along the southeastern foothills of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]], three Kofun GroupsвҖ”{{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|lt=Makimuku Kofun Cluster|lt=Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|зәҸеҗ‘еҸӨеўізҫӨ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to зә’еҗ‘еҸӨеўізҫӨ}}, {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Group|ja|жҹіжң¬еҸӨеўізҫӨ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Group|qq=}}, and Yamato Kofun GroupвҖ”are located in a north-south alignment along the right bank of the {{ill|Hase River (Nara Prefecture)|ja|еҲқзҖ¬е·қ (еҘҲиүҜзңҢ)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hase River (Nara Prefecture)|qq=|1=Hasegawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123408533}}. Although further divided into three smaller groups, they can collectively be referred to as the "ЕҢyamato Kofun Cluster" in its broadest sense.
Excluding the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|зәҸеҗ‘еҸӨеўізҫӨ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Cluster|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to зә’еҗ‘еҸӨеўізҫӨ}} (also known as Hashimoto Kofun Group), which contains early [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] such as [[:en:Hashihaka Kofun|Hashihaka Kofun]], the Yamato Kofun Group (in the narrower sense) and the Yanagimoto Kofun Group are considered to have been constructed slightly later. Together, they are sometimes called the '''ЕҢyamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group'''. These kofun are believed to have been created during the early Kofun period.
== Overview ==
The group is referred to as the "Yamato Kofun Group" due to the presence of the [[:en:ЕҢyamato Shrine|ЕҢyamato Shrine]] at the western edge of the kofun cluster. Within the group, there are 12 known [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], 5 {{ill|ZenpЕҚ-kЕҚhЕҚ-fun|ja|еүҚж–№еҫҢж–№еўі|lt=ZenpЕҚ-kЕҚhЕҚ-fun|1=ZenpЕҚ-kЕҚhЕҚ-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpЕҚ-kЕҚhЕҚ-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ZenpЕҚ-kЕҚhЕҚ-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpЕҚ-kЕҚhЕҚ-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and 7 {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|еҶҶеўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. These tombs can be further divided into two sub-groups: one on the hills (Nakayama subgroup) and one on the alluvial fan (KayЕҚ subgroup). Accordingly, the names "Nakayama Kofun Group" and "KayЕҚ Kofun Group" are often used.
The Yamato Kofun Group lacks significant size differences among the tombs, distinguishing it from the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Group|ja|жҹіжң¬еҸӨеўізҫӨ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Group|qq=}}, which emphasizes primary tombs and subsidiary tombs, and the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|lt=Makimuku Kofun Cluster|lt=Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|зәҸеҗ‘еҸӨеўізҫӨ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to зә’еҗ‘еҸӨеўізҫӨ}}, which also features a primary-subsidiary tomb relationship. Notably, subsidiary tombs do not involve forced burial practices.
The largest keyhole-shaped kofun within the Yamato Kofun Group, the {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|иҘҝж®ҝеЎҡеҸӨеўі|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}} (mound length of 234 meters), is thought to have been constructed earlier than the {{ill|Andon'yama Kofun|ja|иЎҢзҮҲеұұеҸӨеўі|1=Andon-yama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon'yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}} (241 meters, currently designated as the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]) and the {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|жёӢи°·еҗ‘еұұеҸӨеўі|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} (310 meters, currently designated as the tomb of [[:en:Emperor KeikЕҚ|Emperor KeikЕҚ]]) in the Yanagimoto Kofun Group.
Large tombs believed to be royal burial sites moved to the northwestern part of the Nara Basin by the latter half of the early Kofun period, forming the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|ja|дҪҗзҙҖзӣҫеҲ—еҸӨеўізҫӨ|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}}.
In [[:en:2014|2014]] (October 6), the burial areas of the Nomugi Kofun, {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|дёӢжұ еұұеҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikeyama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Nakayama ЕҢtsuka Kofun|ja|дёӯеұұеӨ§еЎҡеҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama ЕҢtsuka Kofun|qq=}} were collectively designated as a National Historic Site under the name "Yamato Kofun Group."<ref>{{еӣҪжҢҮе®ҡж–ҮеҢ–иІЎзӯүгғҮгғјгӮҝгғҷгғјгӮ№|401|00003861|еӨ§е’ҢеҸӨеўізҫӨ|1=Yamato Kofun Group}}</ref>
== Main Kofun ==
The largest tomb in the Yamato Kofun Group is the aforementioned {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|иҘҝж®ҝеЎҡеҸӨеўі|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}}, classified as a "great king tomb." Currently designated as the burial site of [[:en:Princess Tashiraka|Princess Tashiraka]], it is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]], restricting public and academic access without special permission. This designation was made in [[:en:1876|1876]], although Te-shirakano-hime (consort of [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]]) lived during the first half of the [[:en:6th century|6th century]]. However, the estimated construction period of the tomb dates back to the late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]] to the early [[:en:4th century|4th century]], suggesting it served as a royal tomb during the early [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato Kingship]].
Nearby, the {{ill|Higashitonozuka Kofun|ja|жқұж®ҝеЎҡеҸӨеўі|1=Higashitonotsuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashitonotsuka Kofun|qq=}} (175 meters) belongs to the KayЕҚ subgroup and is thought to have been constructed in the early 4th century.
Other significant kofun include seven keyhole-shaped kofun measuring 100вҖ“140 meters and three keyhole-shaped tombs with rectangular backs.
The {{ill|Nakayama ЕҢtsuka Kofun|ja|дёӯеұұеӨ§еЎҡеҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama ЕҢtsuka Kofun|qq=}} (120 meters) contains fragments of {{ill|special pedestal-shaped vessels|ja|зү№ж®ҠеҷЁеҸ°|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/special pedestal-shaped vessels|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to зү№ж®ҠеҷЁеҸ°гғ»зү№ж®ҠеЈә|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570998}}, indicating a possible origin in the early Kofun period, as noted in 1986. In 1993, a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|з«Әз©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=з«Әз©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө|4=zh|5=иұҺз©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} was discovered in the rear circular mound. This chamber features slab stones primarily composed of pyroxene andesite (identified as {{ill|Sanukite|ja|гӮөгғҢгӮ«гӮӨгғҲ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanukite|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to и®ғеІҗеІ©|1=Sanukitoid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q843420}}).
The {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|иҘҝеұұеЎҡеҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}}, which is speculated to be the true tomb of Te-shirakano-hime, is the only tomb within this group dating to the first half of the 6th century.

Along the southeastern foothills of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]], three Kofun GroupsвҖ”{{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|lt=Makimuku Kofun Cluster|lt=Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|зәҸеҗ‘еҸӨеўізҫӨ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to зә’еҗ‘еҸӨеўізҫӨ}}, {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Group|ja|жҹіжң¬еҸӨеўізҫӨ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Group|qq=}}, and Yamato Kofun GroupвҖ”are located in a north-south alignment along the right bank of the {{ill|Hase River (Nara Prefecture)|ja|еҲқзҖ¬е·қ (еҘҲиүҜзңҢ)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hase River (Nara Prefecture)|qq=|1=Hasegawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123408533}}. Although further divided into three smaller groups, they can collectively be referred to as the "ЕҢyamato Kofun Cluster" in its broadest sense.
Excluding the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|зәҸеҗ‘еҸӨеўізҫӨ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Cluster|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to зә’еҗ‘еҸӨеўізҫӨ}} (also known as Hashimoto Kofun Group), which contains early [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] such as [[:en:Hashihaka Kofun|Hashihaka Kofun]], the Yamato Kofun Group (in the narrower sense) and the Yanagimoto Kofun Group are considered to have been constructed slightly later. Together, they are sometimes called the '''ЕҢyamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group'''. These kofun are believed to have been created during the early Kofun period.
== Overview ==
The group is referred to as the "Yamato Kofun Group" due to the presence of the [[:en:ЕҢyamato Shrine|ЕҢyamato Shrine]] at the western edge of the kofun cluster. Within the group, there are 12 known [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], 5 {{ill|ZenpЕҚ-kЕҚhЕҚ-fun|ja|еүҚж–№еҫҢж–№еўі|lt=ZenpЕҚ-kЕҚhЕҚ-fun|1=ZenpЕҚ-kЕҚhЕҚ-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpЕҚ-kЕҚhЕҚ-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ZenpЕҚ-kЕҚhЕҚ-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpЕҚ-kЕҚhЕҚ-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and 7 {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|еҶҶеўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. These tombs can be further divided into two sub-groups: one on the hills (Nakayama subgroup) and one on the alluvial fan (KayЕҚ subgroup). Accordingly, the names "Nakayama Kofun Group" and "KayЕҚ Kofun Group" are often used.
The Yamato Kofun Group lacks significant size differences among the tombs, distinguishing it from the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Group|ja|жҹіжң¬еҸӨеўізҫӨ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Group|qq=}}, which emphasizes primary tombs and subsidiary tombs, and the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|lt=Makimuku Kofun Cluster|lt=Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|зәҸеҗ‘еҸӨеўізҫӨ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to зә’еҗ‘еҸӨеўізҫӨ}}, which also features a primary-subsidiary tomb relationship. Notably, subsidiary tombs do not involve forced burial practices.
The largest keyhole-shaped kofun within the Yamato Kofun Group, the {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|иҘҝж®ҝеЎҡеҸӨеўі|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}} (mound length of 234 meters), is thought to have been constructed earlier than the {{ill|Andon'yama Kofun|ja|иЎҢзҮҲеұұеҸӨеўі|1=Andon-yama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon'yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}} (241 meters, currently designated as the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]) and the {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|жёӢи°·еҗ‘еұұеҸӨеўі|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} (310 meters, currently designated as the tomb of [[:en:Emperor KeikЕҚ|Emperor KeikЕҚ]]) in the Yanagimoto Kofun Group.
Large tombs believed to be royal burial sites moved to the northwestern part of the Nara Basin by the latter half of the early Kofun period, forming the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|ja|дҪҗзҙҖзӣҫеҲ—еҸӨеўізҫӨ|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}}.
In [[:en:2014|2014]] (October 6), the burial areas of the Nomugi Kofun, {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|дёӢжұ еұұеҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikeyama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Nakayama ЕҢtsuka Kofun|ja|дёӯеұұеӨ§еЎҡеҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama ЕҢtsuka Kofun|qq=}} were collectively designated as a National Historic Site under the name "Yamato Kofun Group."<ref>{{еӣҪжҢҮе®ҡж–ҮеҢ–иІЎзӯүгғҮгғјгӮҝгғҷгғјгӮ№|401|00003861|еӨ§е’ҢеҸӨеўізҫӨ|1=Yamato Kofun Group}}</ref>
== Main Kofun ==
The largest tomb in the Yamato Kofun Group is the aforementioned {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|иҘҝж®ҝеЎҡеҸӨеўі|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}}, classified as a "great king tomb." Currently designated as the burial site of [[:en:Princess Tashiraka|Princess Tashiraka]], it is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]], restricting public and academic access without special permission. This designation was made in [[:en:1876|1876]], although Te-shirakano-hime (consort of [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]]) lived during the first half of the [[:en:6th century|6th century]]. However, the estimated construction period of the tomb dates back to the late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]] to the early [[:en:4th century|4th century]], suggesting it served as a royal tomb during the early [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato Kingship]].
Nearby, the {{ill|Higashitonozuka Kofun|ja|жқұж®ҝеЎҡеҸӨеўі|1=Higashitonotsuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashitonotsuka Kofun|qq=}} (175 meters) belongs to the KayЕҚ subgroup and is thought to have been constructed in the early 4th century.
Other significant kofun include seven keyhole-shaped kofun measuring 100вҖ“140 meters and three keyhole-shaped tombs with rectangular backs.
The {{ill|Nakayama ЕҢtsuka Kofun|ja|дёӯеұұеӨ§еЎҡеҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama ЕҢtsuka Kofun|qq=}} (120 meters) contains fragments of {{ill|special pedestal-shaped vessels|ja|зү№ж®ҠеҷЁеҸ°|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/special pedestal-shaped vessels|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to зү№ж®ҠеҷЁеҸ°гғ»зү№ж®ҠеЈә|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570998}}, indicating a possible origin in the early Kofun period, as noted in 1986. In 1993, a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|з«Әз©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=з«Әз©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө|4=zh|5=иұҺз©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} was discovered in the rear circular mound. This chamber features slab stones primarily composed of pyroxene andesite (identified as {{ill|Sanukite|ja|гӮөгғҢгӮ«гӮӨгғҲ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanukite|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to и®ғеІҗеІ©|1=Sanukitoid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q843420}}).
The {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|иҘҝеұұеЎҡеҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}}, which is speculated to be the true tomb of Te-shirakano-hime, is the only tomb within this group dating to the first half of the 6th century.

It is also referred to as "'''ЕҢyamato Kofun Cluster'''." The largest kofun is {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|иҘҝж®ҝеЎҡеҸӨеўі|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}}. Along the southeastern foothills of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]], three Kofun GroupsвҖ”{{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|lt=Makimuku Kofun Cluster|lt=Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|зәҸеҗ‘еҸӨеўізҫӨ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to зә’еҗ‘еҸӨеўізҫӨ}}, {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Group|ja|жҹіжң¬еҸӨеўізҫӨ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Group|qq=}}, and Yamato Kofun GroupвҖ”are located in a north-south alignment along the right bank of the {{ill|Hase River (Nara Prefecture)|ja|еҲқзҖ¬е·қ (еҘҲиүҜзңҢ)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hase River (Nara Prefecture)|1=Hasegawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123408533}}. Although further divided into three smaller groups, they can collectively be referred to as the "ЕҢyamato Kofun Cluster" in its broadest sense.
Excluding the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|зәҸеҗ‘еҸӨеўізҫӨ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to зә’еҗ‘еҸӨеўізҫӨ}} (also known as Hashimoto Kofun Group), which contains early [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] such as [[:en:Hashihaka Kofun|Hashihaka Kofun]], the Yamato Kofun Group (in the narrower sense) and the Yanagimoto Kofun Group are considered to have been constructed slightly later. Together, they are sometimes called the '''ЕҢyamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group'''. These kofun are believed to have been created during the early Kofun period.

The largest keyhole-shaped kofun within the Yamato Kofun Group, the {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|иҘҝж®ҝеЎҡеҸӨеўі|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}} (mound length of 234 meters), is thought to have been constructed earlier than the {{ill|Andon'yama Kofun|ja|иЎҢзҮҲеұұеҸӨеўі|1=Andon-yama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon'yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}} (241 meters, currently designated as the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]) and the {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|жёӢи°·еҗ‘еұұеҸӨеўі|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} (310 meters, currently designated as the tomb of [[:en:Emperor KeikЕҚ|Emperor KeikЕҚ]]) in the Yanagimoto Kofun Group.

== Main Kofun ==
The largest tomb in the Yamato Kofun Group is the aforementioned {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|иҘҝж®ҝеЎҡеҸӨеўі|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}}, classified as a "great king tomb." Currently designated as the burial site of [[:en:Princess Tashiraka|Princess Tashiraka]], it is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]], restricting public and academic access without special permission. This designation was made in [[:en:1876|1876]], although Te-shirakano-hime (consort of [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]]) lived during the first half of the [[:en:6th century|6th century]]. However, the estimated construction period of the tomb dates back to the late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]] to the early [[:en:4th century|4th century]], suggesting it served as a royal tomb during the early [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato Kingship]].

[[ja:еӨ§е’ҢеҸӨеўізҫӨ]] [[uk:РЎРәСғРҝСҮРөРҪРҪСҸ РәСғСҖРіР°РҪС–РІ РһСҸРјР°СӮРҫ]]
<!--Yamato Kofun Group--><!--Yamato Kofun Group--><!--Yamato Kofun Group--><!--Yamato Kofun Group--><!--Yamato Kofun Group--><!--Yamato Kofun Group--><!--Yamato Kofun Group--><!--Yamato Kofun Group--> [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:ЕҢyamato Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:History of Tenri, Nara]]
[[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]]
{{wikidata link|Q11433419}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:еӨ§е’ҢеҸӨеўізҫӨ]][[uk:РЎРәСғРҝСҮРөРҪРҪСҸ РәСғСҖРіР°РҪС–РІ РһСҸРјР°СӮРҫ]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tenri|WD=Q502196}}|part_of={{ill|Hiezuka Kofun|WD=Q30923506}}; {{ill|Higashitonozuka Kofun|WD=Q11527550}}; {{ill|Nakayama ЕҢtsuka Kofun|WD=Q11364125}}; {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|WD=Q11628944}}; {{ill|Nishiyamazuka Kofun|WD=Q11627823}}; {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|WD=Q11361070}}|Historic Site={{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11433419]] Wikidata name: ЕҢyamato Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:42, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Yamato Kofun Group''' (еӨ§е’ҢеҸӨеўізҫӨ) is a [[Kofun Group]] located in the southern part of [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara Prefecture]] (иҗұз”ҹз”ә to дёӯеұұз”ә). Some parts of the group have been designated as [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}.
It is also referred to as "'''ЕҢyamato Kofun Cluster'''." The largest kofun is {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|иҘҝж®ҝеЎҡеҸӨеўі|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}}. Along the southeastern foothills of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]], three Kofun GroupsвҖ”{{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|lt=Makimuku Kofun Cluster|lt=Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|зәҸеҗ‘еҸӨеўізҫӨ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to зә’еҗ‘еҸӨеўізҫӨ}}, {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Group|ja|жҹіжң¬еҸӨеўізҫӨ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Group|qq=}}, and Yamato Kofun GroupвҖ”are located in a north-south alignment along the right bank of the {{ill|Hase River (Nara Prefecture)|ja|еҲқзҖ¬е·қ (еҘҲиүҜзңҢ)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hase River (Nara Prefecture)|1=Hasegawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123408533}}. Although further divided into three smaller groups, they can collectively be referred to as the "ЕҢyamato Kofun Cluster" in its broadest sense.
Excluding the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|зәҸеҗ‘еҸӨеўізҫӨ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to зә’еҗ‘еҸӨеўізҫӨ}} (also known as Hashimoto Kofun Group), which contains early [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] such as [[:en:Hashihaka Kofun|Hashihaka Kofun]], the Yamato Kofun Group (in the narrower sense) and the Yanagimoto Kofun Group are considered to have been constructed slightly later. Together, they are sometimes called the '''ЕҢyamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group'''. These kofun are believed to have been created during the early Kofun period.
== Overview ==
The group is referred to as the "Yamato Kofun Group" due to the presence of the [[:en:ЕҢyamato Shrine|ЕҢyamato Shrine]] at the western edge of the kofun cluster. Within the group, there are 12 known [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], 5 {{ill|ZenpЕҚ-kЕҚhЕҚ-fun|ja|еүҚж–№еҫҢж–№еўі|lt=ZenpЕҚ-kЕҚhЕҚ-fun|1=ZenpЕҚ-kЕҚhЕҚ-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpЕҚ-kЕҚhЕҚ-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ZenpЕҚ-kЕҚhЕҚ-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpЕҚ-kЕҚhЕҚ-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and 7 {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|еҶҶеўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. These tombs can be further divided into two sub-groups: one on the hills (Nakayama subgroup) and one on the alluvial fan (KayЕҚ subgroup). Accordingly, the names "Nakayama Kofun Group" and "KayЕҚ Kofun Group" are often used.
The Yamato Kofun Group lacks significant size differences among the tombs, distinguishing it from the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Group|ja|жҹіжң¬еҸӨеўізҫӨ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Group|qq=}}, which emphasizes primary tombs and subsidiary tombs, and the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|lt=Makimuku Kofun Cluster|lt=Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|зәҸеҗ‘еҸӨеўізҫӨ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to зә’еҗ‘еҸӨеўізҫӨ}}, which also features a primary-subsidiary tomb relationship. Notably, subsidiary tombs do not involve forced burial practices.
The largest keyhole-shaped kofun within the Yamato Kofun Group, the {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|иҘҝж®ҝеЎҡеҸӨеўі|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}} (mound length of 234 meters), is thought to have been constructed earlier than the {{ill|Andon'yama Kofun|ja|иЎҢзҮҲеұұеҸӨеўі|1=Andon-yama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon'yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}} (241 meters, currently designated as the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]) and the {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|жёӢи°·еҗ‘еұұеҸӨеўі|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} (310 meters, currently designated as the tomb of [[:en:Emperor KeikЕҚ|Emperor KeikЕҚ]]) in the Yanagimoto Kofun Group.
Large tombs believed to be royal burial sites moved to the northwestern part of the Nara Basin by the latter half of the early Kofun period, forming the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|ja|дҪҗзҙҖзӣҫеҲ—еҸӨеўізҫӨ|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}}.
In [[:en:2014|2014]] (October 6), the burial areas of the Nomugi Kofun, {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|дёӢжұ еұұеҸӨеўі|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikeyama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Nakayama ЕҢtsuka Kofun|ja|дёӯеұұеӨ§еЎҡеҸӨеўі|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama ЕҢtsuka Kofun|qq=}} were collectively designated as a National Historic Site under the name "Yamato Kofun Group."<ref>{{еӣҪжҢҮе®ҡж–ҮеҢ–иІЎзӯүгғҮгғјгӮҝгғҷгғјгӮ№|401|00003861|еӨ§е’ҢеҸӨеўізҫӨ|1=Yamato Kofun Group}}</ref>
== Main Kofun ==
The largest tomb in the Yamato Kofun Group is the aforementioned {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|иҘҝж®ҝеЎҡеҸӨеўі|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}}, classified as a "great king tomb." Currently designated as the burial site of [[:en:Princess Tashiraka|Princess Tashiraka]], it is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]], restricting public and academic access without special permission. This designation was made in [[:en:1876|1876]], although Te-shirakano-hime (consort of [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]]) lived during the first half of the [[:en:6th century|6th century]]. However, the estimated construction period of the tomb dates back to the late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]] to the early [[:en:4th century|4th century]], suggesting it served as a royal tomb during the early [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato Kingship]].
Nearby, the {{ill|Higashitonozuka Kofun|ja|жқұж®ҝеЎҡеҸӨеўі|1=Higashitonotsuka Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashitonotsuka Kofun|qq=}} (175 meters) belongs to the KayЕҚ subgroup and is thought to have been constructed in the early 4th century.
Other significant kofun include seven keyhole-shaped kofun measuring 100вҖ“140 meters and three keyhole-shaped tombs with rectangular backs.
The {{ill|Nakayama ЕҢtsuka Kofun|ja|дёӯеұұеӨ§еЎҡеҸӨеўі|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama ЕҢtsuka Kofun|qq=}} (120 meters) contains fragments of {{ill|special pedestal-shaped vessels|ja|зү№ж®ҠеҷЁеҸ°|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/special pedestal-shaped vessels|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to зү№ж®ҠеҷЁеҸ°гғ»зү№ж®ҠеЈә|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570998}}, indicating a possible origin in the early Kofun period, as noted in 1986. In 1993, a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|з«Әз©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=з«Әз©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө|4=zh|5=иұҺз©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} was discovered in the rear circular mound. This chamber features slab stones primarily composed of pyroxene andesite (identified as {{ill|Sanukite|ja|гӮөгғҢгӮ«гӮӨгғҲ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanukite|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to и®ғеІҗеІ©|1=Sanukitoid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q843420}}).
The {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|иҘҝеұұеЎҡеҸӨеўі|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}}, which is speculated to be the true tomb of Te-shirakano-hime, is the only tomb within this group dating to the first half of the 6th century.

Other significant kofun include seven keyhole-shaped kofun measuring 100вҖ“140 meters and three keyhole-shaped tombs with rectangular backs.
The {{ill|Nakayama ЕҢtsuka Kofun|ja|дёӯеұұеӨ§еЎҡеҸӨеўі|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama ЕҢtsuka Kofun|qq=}} (120 meters) contains fragments of {{ill|special pedestal-shaped vessels|ja|зү№ж®ҠеҷЁеҸ°|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/special pedestal-shaped vessels|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to зү№ж®ҠеҷЁеҸ°гғ»зү№ж®ҠеЈә|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570998}}, indicating a possible origin in the early Kofun period, as noted in 1986. In 1993, a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|з«Әз©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=з«Әз©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө|4=zh|5=иұҺз©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} was discovered in the rear circular mound. This chamber features slab stones primarily composed of pyroxene andesite (identified as {{ill|Sanukite|ja|гӮөгғҢгӮ«гӮӨгғҲ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanukite|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to и®ғеІҗеІ©|1=Sanukitoid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q843420}}).
The {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|иҘҝеұұеЎҡеҸӨеўі|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}}, which is speculated to be the true tomb of Te-shirakano-hime, is the only tomb within this group dating to the first half of the 6th century.

revision.comment: Bot: Add Wikidata no short description - based on Wikidata English short description
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:еӨ§е’ҢеҸӨеўізҫӨ]][[uk:РЎРәСғРҝСҮРөРҪРҪСҸ РәСғСҖРіР°РҪС–РІ РһСҸРјР°СӮРҫ]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tenri|WD=Q502196}}|part_of={{ill|Hiezuka Kofun|WD=Q30923506}}; {{ill|Higashitonozuka Kofun|WD=Q11527550}}; {{ill|Nakayama ЕҢtsuka Kofun|WD=Q11364125}}; {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|WD=Q11628944}}; {{ill|Nishiyamazuka Kofun|WD=Q11627823}}; {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|WD=Q11361070}}|Historic Site={{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11433419]] Wikidata name: ЕҢyamato Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:42, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Yamato Kofun Group''' (еӨ§е’ҢеҸӨеўізҫӨ) is a [[Kofun Group]] located in the southern part of [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara Prefecture]] (иҗұз”ҹз”ә to дёӯеұұз”ә). Some parts of the group have been designated as [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}.
It is also referred to as "'''ЕҢyamato Kofun Cluster'''." The largest kofun is {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|иҘҝж®ҝеЎҡеҸӨеўі|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}}. Along the southeastern foothills of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]], three Kofun GroupsвҖ”{{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|lt=Makimuku Kofun Cluster|lt=Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|зәҸеҗ‘еҸӨеўізҫӨ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to зә’еҗ‘еҸӨеўізҫӨ}}, {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Group|ja|жҹіжң¬еҸӨеўізҫӨ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Group|qq=}}, and Yamato Kofun GroupвҖ”are located in a north-south alignment along the right bank of the {{ill|Hase River (Nara Prefecture)|ja|еҲқзҖ¬е·қ (еҘҲиүҜзңҢ)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hase River (Nara Prefecture)|1=Hasegawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123408533}}. Although further divided into three smaller groups, they can collectively be referred to as the "ЕҢyamato Kofun Cluster" in its broadest sense.
Excluding the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|зәҸеҗ‘еҸӨеўізҫӨ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to зә’еҗ‘еҸӨеўізҫӨ}} (also known as Hashimoto Kofun Group), which contains early [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] such as [[:en:Hashihaka Kofun|Hashihaka Kofun]], the Yamato Kofun Group (in the narrower sense) and the Yanagimoto Kofun Group are considered to have been constructed slightly later. Together, they are sometimes called the '''ЕҢyamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group'''. These kofun are believed to have been created during the early Kofun period.
== Overview ==
The group is referred to as the "Yamato Kofun Group" due to the presence of the [[:en:ЕҢyamato Shrine|ЕҢyamato Shrine]] at the western edge of the kofun cluster. Within the group, there are 12 known [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], 5 {{ill|ZenpЕҚ-kЕҚhЕҚ-fun|ja|еүҚж–№еҫҢж–№еўі|lt=ZenpЕҚ-kЕҚhЕҚ-fun|1=ZenpЕҚ-kЕҚhЕҚ-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpЕҚ-kЕҚhЕҚ-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ZenpЕҚ-kЕҚhЕҚ-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpЕҚ-kЕҚhЕҚ-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and 7 {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|еҶҶеўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. These tombs can be further divided into two sub-groups: one on the hills (Nakayama subgroup) and one on the alluvial fan (KayЕҚ subgroup). Accordingly, the names "Nakayama Kofun Group" and "KayЕҚ Kofun Group" are often used.
The Yamato Kofun Group lacks significant size differences among the tombs, distinguishing it from the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Group|ja|жҹіжң¬еҸӨеўізҫӨ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Group|qq=}}, which emphasizes primary tombs and subsidiary tombs, and the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|lt=Makimuku Kofun Cluster|lt=Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|зәҸеҗ‘еҸӨеўізҫӨ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to зә’еҗ‘еҸӨеўізҫӨ}}, which also features a primary-subsidiary tomb relationship. Notably, subsidiary tombs do not involve forced burial practices.
The largest keyhole-shaped kofun within the Yamato Kofun Group, the {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|иҘҝж®ҝеЎҡеҸӨеўі|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}} (mound length of 234 meters), is thought to have been constructed earlier than the {{ill|Andon'yama Kofun|ja|иЎҢзҮҲеұұеҸӨеўі|1=Andon-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon'yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}} (241 meters, currently designated as the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]) and the {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|жёӢи°·еҗ‘еұұеҸӨеўі|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} (310 meters, currently designated as the tomb of [[:en:Emperor KeikЕҚ|Emperor KeikЕҚ]]) in the Yanagimoto Kofun Group.
Large tombs believed to be royal burial sites moved to the northwestern part of the Nara Basin by the latter half of the early Kofun period, forming the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|ja|дҪҗзҙҖзӣҫеҲ—еҸӨеўізҫӨ|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}}.
In [[:en:2014|2014]] (October 6), the burial areas of the Nomugi Kofun, {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|дёӢжұ еұұеҸӨеўі|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikeyama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Nakayama ЕҢtsuka Kofun|ja|дёӯеұұеӨ§еЎҡеҸӨеўі|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama ЕҢtsuka Kofun|qq=}} were collectively designated as a National Historic Site under the name "Yamato Kofun Group."<ref>{{еӣҪжҢҮе®ҡж–ҮеҢ–иІЎзӯүгғҮгғјгӮҝгғҷгғјгӮ№|401|00003861|еӨ§е’ҢеҸӨеўізҫӨ|1=Yamato Kofun Group}}</ref>
== Main Kofun ==
The largest tomb in the Yamato Kofun Group is the aforementioned {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|иҘҝж®ҝеЎҡеҸӨеўі|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}}, classified as a "great king tomb." Currently designated as the burial site of [[:en:Princess Tashiraka|Princess Tashiraka]], it is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]], restricting public and academic access without special permission. This designation was made in [[:en:1876|1876]], although Te-shirakano-hime (consort of [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]]) lived during the first half of the [[:en:6th century|6th century]]. However, the estimated construction period of the tomb dates back to the late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]] to the early [[:en:4th century|4th century]], suggesting it served as a royal tomb during the early [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato Kingship]].
Nearby, the {{ill|Higashitonozuka Kofun|ja|жқұж®ҝеЎҡеҸӨеўі|1=Higashitonotsuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashitonotsuka Kofun|qq=}} (175 meters) belongs to the KayЕҚ subgroup and is thought to have been constructed in the early 4th century.
Other significant kofun include seven keyhole-shaped kofun measuring 100вҖ“140 meters and three keyhole-shaped tombs with rectangular backs.
The {{ill|Nakayama ЕҢtsuka Kofun|ja|дёӯеұұеӨ§еЎҡеҸӨеўі|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama ЕҢtsuka Kofun|qq=}} (120 meters) contains fragments of {{ill|special pedestal-shaped vessels|ja|зү№ж®ҠеҷЁеҸ°|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/special pedestal-shaped vessels|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to зү№ж®ҠеҷЁеҸ°гғ»зү№ж®ҠеЈә|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570998}}, indicating a possible origin in the early Kofun period, as noted in 1986. In 1993, a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|з«Әз©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=з«Әз©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө|4=zh|5=иұҺз©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} was discovered in the rear circular mound. This chamber features slab stones primarily composed of pyroxene andesite (identified as {{ill|Sanukite|ja|гӮөгғҢгӮ«гӮӨгғҲ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanukite|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to и®ғеІҗеІ©|1=Sanukitoid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q843420}}).
The {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|иҘҝеұұеЎҡеҸӨеўі|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}}, which is speculated to be the true tomb of Te-shirakano-hime, is the only tomb within this group dating to the first half of the 6th century.

The largest keyhole-shaped kofun within the Yamato Kofun Group, the {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|иҘҝж®ҝеЎҡеҸӨеўі|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}} (mound length of 234 meters), is thought to have been constructed earlier than the {{ill|Andon'yama Kofun|ja|иЎҢзҮҲеұұеҸӨеўі|1=Andon-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon'yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}} (241 meters, currently designated as the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]) and the {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|жёӢи°·еҗ‘еұұеҸӨеўі|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} (310 meters, currently designated as the tomb of [[:en:Emperor KeikЕҚ|Emperor KeikЕҚ]]) in the Yanagimoto Kofun Group.
Large tombs believed to be royal burial sites moved to the northwestern part of the Nara Basin by the latter half of the early Kofun period, forming the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|ja|дҪҗзҙҖзӣҫеҲ—еҸӨеўізҫӨ|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}}.
In [[:en:2014|2014]] (October 6), the burial areas of the Nomugi Kofun, {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|дёӢжұ еұұеҸӨеўі|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikeyama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Nakayama ЕҢtsuka Kofun|ja|дёӯеұұеӨ§еЎҡеҸӨеўі|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama ЕҢtsuka Kofun|qq=}} were collectively designated as a National Historic Site under the name "Yamato Kofun Group."<ref>{{еӣҪжҢҮе®ҡж–ҮеҢ–иІЎзӯүгғҮгғјгӮҝгғҷгғјгӮ№|401|00003861|еӨ§е’ҢеҸӨеўізҫӨ|1=Yamato Kofun Group}}</ref>
== Main Kofun ==
The largest tomb in the Yamato Kofun Group is the aforementioned {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|иҘҝж®ҝеЎҡеҸӨеўі|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}}, classified as a "great king tomb." Currently designated as the burial site of [[:en:Princess Tashiraka|Princess Tashiraka]], it is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]], restricting public and academic access without special permission. This designation was made in [[:en:1876|1876]], although Te-shirakano-hime (consort of [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]]) lived during the first half of the [[:en:6th century|6th century]]. However, the estimated construction period of the tomb dates back to the late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]] to the early [[:en:4th century|4th century]], suggesting it served as a royal tomb during the early [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato Kingship]].
Nearby, the {{ill|Higashitonozuka Kofun|ja|жқұж®ҝеЎҡеҸӨеўі|1=Higashitonotsuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashitonotsuka Kofun|qq=}} (175 meters) belongs to the KayЕҚ subgroup and is thought to have been constructed in the early 4th century.
Other significant kofun include seven keyhole-shaped kofun measuring 100вҖ“140 meters and three keyhole-shaped tombs with rectangular backs.
The {{ill|Nakayama ЕҢtsuka Kofun|ja|дёӯеұұеӨ§еЎҡеҸӨеўі|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama ЕҢtsuka Kofun|qq=}} (120 meters) contains fragments of {{ill|special pedestal-shaped vessels|ja|зү№ж®ҠеҷЁеҸ°|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/special pedestal-shaped vessels|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to зү№ж®ҠеҷЁеҸ°гғ»зү№ж®ҠеЈә|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570998}}, indicating a possible origin in the early Kofun period, as noted in 1986. In 1993, a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|з«Әз©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=з«Әз©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө|4=zh|5=иұҺз©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} was discovered in the rear circular mound. This chamber features slab stones primarily composed of pyroxene andesite (identified as {{ill|Sanukite|ja|гӮөгғҢгӮ«гӮӨгғҲ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanukite|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to и®ғеІҗеІ©|1=Sanukitoid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q843420}}).
The {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|иҘҝеұұеЎҡеҸӨеўі|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}}, which is speculated to be the true tomb of Te-shirakano-hime, is the only tomb within this group dating to the first half of the 6th century.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:еӨ§е’ҢеҸӨеўізҫӨ]][[uk:РЎРәСғРҝСҮРөРҪРҪСҸ РәСғСҖРіР°РҪС–РІ РһСҸРјР°СӮРҫ]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tenri|WD=Q502196}}|part_of={{ill|Hiezuka Kofun|WD=Q30923506}}; {{ill|Higashitonozuka Kofun|WD=Q11527550}}; {{ill|Nakayama ЕҢtsuka Kofun|WD=Q11364125}}; {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|WD=Q11628944}}; {{ill|Nishiyamazuka Kofun|WD=Q11627823}}; {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|WD=Q11361070}}|Historic Site={{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11433419]] Wikidata name: ЕҢyamato Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:42, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Yamato Kofun Group''' (еӨ§е’ҢеҸӨеўізҫӨ) is a [[Kofun Group]] located in the southern part of [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara Prefecture]] (иҗұз”ҹз”ә to дёӯеұұз”ә). Some parts of the group have been designated as [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}.
It is also referred to as "'''ЕҢyamato Kofun Cluster'''." The largest kofun is {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|иҘҝж®ҝеЎҡеҸӨеўі|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}}. Along the southeastern foothills of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]], three Kofun GroupsвҖ”{{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|lt=Makimuku Kofun Cluster|lt=Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|зәҸеҗ‘еҸӨеўізҫӨ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to зә’еҗ‘еҸӨеўізҫӨ}}, {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Group|ja|жҹіжң¬еҸӨеўізҫӨ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Group|qq=}}, and Yamato Kofun GroupвҖ”are located in a north-south alignment along the right bank of the {{ill|Hase River (Nara Prefecture)|ja|еҲқзҖ¬е·қ (еҘҲиүҜзңҢ)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hase River (Nara Prefecture)|1=Hasegawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123408533}}. Although further divided into three smaller groups, they can collectively be referred to as the "ЕҢyamato Kofun Cluster" in its broadest sense.
Excluding the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|зәҸеҗ‘еҸӨеўізҫӨ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to зә’еҗ‘еҸӨеўізҫӨ}} (also known as Hashimoto Kofun Group), which contains early [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] such as [[:en:Hashihaka Kofun|Hashihaka Kofun]], the Yamato Kofun Group (in the narrower sense) and the Yanagimoto Kofun Group are considered to have been constructed slightly later. Together, they are sometimes called the '''ЕҢyamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group'''. These kofun are believed to have been created during the early Kofun period.
== Overview ==
The group is referred to as the "Yamato Kofun Group" due to the presence of the [[:en:ЕҢyamato Shrine|ЕҢyamato Shrine]] at the western edge of the kofun cluster. Within the group, there are 12 known [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], 5 {{ill|ZenpЕҚ-kЕҚhЕҚ-fun|ja|еүҚж–№еҫҢж–№еўі|lt=ZenpЕҚ-kЕҚhЕҚ-fun|1=ZenpЕҚ-kЕҚhЕҚ-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpЕҚ-kЕҚhЕҚ-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ZenpЕҚ-kЕҚhЕҚ-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpЕҚ-kЕҚhЕҚ-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and 7 {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|еҶҶеўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. These tombs can be further divided into two sub-groups: one on the hills (Nakayama subgroup) and one on the alluvial fan (KayЕҚ subgroup). Accordingly, the names "Nakayama Kofun Group" and "KayЕҚ Kofun Group" are often used.
The Yamato Kofun Group lacks significant size differences among the tombs, distinguishing it from the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Group|ja|жҹіжң¬еҸӨеўізҫӨ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Group|qq=}}, which emphasizes primary tombs and subsidiary tombs, and the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|lt=Makimuku Kofun Cluster|lt=Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|зәҸеҗ‘еҸӨеўізҫӨ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to зә’еҗ‘еҸӨеўізҫӨ}}, which also features a primary-subsidiary tomb relationship. Notably, subsidiary tombs do not involve forced burial practices.
The largest keyhole-shaped kofun within the Yamato Kofun Group, the {{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|иҘҝж®ҝеЎҡеҸӨеўі|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}} (mound length of 234 meters), is thought to have been constructed earlier than the {{ill|Andon'yama Kofun|ja|иЎҢзҮҲеұұеҸӨеўі|1=Andon-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon'yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}} (241 meters, currently designated as the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]) and the {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|жёӢи°·еҗ‘еұұеҸӨеўі|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} (310 meters, currently designated as the tomb of [[:en:Emperor KeikЕҚ|Emperor KeikЕҚ]]) in the Yanagimoto Kofun Group.
Large tombs believed to be royal burial sites moved to the northwestern part of the Nara Basin by the latter half of the early Kofun period, forming the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|ja|дҪҗзҙҖзӣҫеҲ—еҸӨеўізҫӨ|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}}.
In [[:en:2014|2014]] (October 6), the burial areas of the Nomugi Kofun, {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|дёӢжұ еұұеҸӨеўі|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikeyama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Nakayama ЕҢtsuka Kofun|ja|дёӯеұұеӨ§еЎҡеҸӨеўі|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama ЕҢtsuka Kofun|qq=}} were collectively designated as a National Historic Site under the name "Yamato Kofun Group."<ref>{{еӣҪжҢҮе®ҡж–ҮеҢ–иІЎзӯүгғҮгғјгӮҝгғҷгғјгӮ№|401|00003861|еӨ§е’ҢеҸӨеўізҫӨ|1=Yamato Kofun Group}}</ref>

However, the estimated construction period of the tomb dates back to the late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]] to the early [[:en:4th century|4th century]], suggesting it served as a royal tomb during the early [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato Kingship]].

The {{ill|Nakayama ЕҢtsuka Kofun|ja|дёӯеұұеӨ§еЎҡеҸӨеўі|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama ЕҢtsuka Kofun|qq=}} (120 meters) contains fragments of {{ill|special pedestal-shaped vessels|ja|зү№ж®ҠеҷЁеҸ°|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/special pedestal-shaped vessels|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to зү№ж®ҠеҷЁеҸ°гғ»зү№ж®ҠеЈә|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570998}}, indicating a possible origin in the early Kofun period, as noted in 1986. This chamber features slab stones primarily composed of pyroxene andesite (identified as {{ill|Sanukite|ja|гӮөгғҢгӮ«гӮӨгғҲ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanukite|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to и®ғеІҗеІ©|1=Sanukitoid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q843420}}).
The {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|иҘҝеұұеЎҡеҸӨеўі|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}}, which is speculated to be the true tomb of Te-shirakano-hime, is the only tomb within this group dating to the first half of the 6th century.
